key_insights.insight: Values are truly reflected in where you spend your free time and discretionary money, not just what you say you value.
key_insights.quote: What I've noticed is that where we spend that free time,where we spend our discretionary money, that's what we value.

key_insights.insight: Arranged marriages can be successful due to the village of support making decisions, and because people are not often in a state of true love when initially partnering.
key_insights.quote: What's happening in most arranged marriages is you have a village...who have your best interest and they sit and they cohesively decide, is this the right partner for you?

key_insights.insight: Entering a relationship already satisfied in life is a key predictor of satisfaction within the relationship.
key_insights.quote: If you walkinto a relationship and you have a high level of satisfaction, that is what gives you the highestlevel of satisfaction in the relationship.

key_insights.insight: A primary reason for relationship failure is selecting the wrong partner in the first place, and sometimes letting oneself be chosen instead of making an active choice.
key_insights.quote: I fundamentally believe that the number one reason for breakups is not...it's we pick bad partners. We select the wrong partners or we don't make the selection at all.

key_insights.insight: Good love is often boring from an outside perspective but feels fulfilling internally.
key_insights.quote: Great love looks boring, but it feels great.

key_insights.insight: Many men struggle with dating due to fear of rejection, which stems from self-esteem and historical mating dynamics.
key_insights.quote: The majority of men report the number one reason why they believethey can't find a significant other is the fear of being rejected.

key_insights.insight: High self-esteem allows individuals to see the value in others beyond traditional attractiveness and to require less external validation, influencing mate selection.
key_insights.quote: The higher self-esteem you have, the less you need to have someone who's traditionally attractive...You don't need to walk down thestreet and have everyone say, he looks like he's so beautiful. She's so beautiful.

key_insights.insight: Authenticity wins in dating; showcasing imperfections and vulnerabilities can be attractive.
key_insights.quote: What the data is showing us more than ever is that authenticityis what wins. Leaning into your quirks, leaning into the things that make you extraordinary.

key_insights.insight: While many superficial characteristics are listed when seeking a partner, there are five key traits to look for: emotional fitness, courageous vision, resilient resourcefulness, open-minded understanding, and compassionate support.
key_insights.quote: There's five key characteristics that we need: emotionally fit, courageous vision, resiliently resourceful, open-minded understanding, compassionate support.

actionable_advice.how_to: Engage in journaling, meditation, or exercise.

actionable_advice.advice: Set and achieve small, attainable goals to build self-esteem and habits.
actionable_advice.how_to: Commit to waking up at a specific time and consistently follow through.
For men, try shoulder press exercises or wear wide-shouldered blazers.
Emotional fitness, Courageous Vision, Resilient Resourcefulness, Open-minded understanding, Compassionate Support
Learn to actively listen and respond versus reacting to your partner.

actionable_advice.advice: Go back and understand the trauma of your partner.
actionable_advice.how_to: Dialogue on each other's experiences to know the why.

memorable_stories: Anecdote about friend looking for a partner based on appearance and income but missing great people.
Story about black women on dating apps not getting the same matches.
Anecdote about a friend who had to move to London.
contrarian_claims: Arranged marriages can yield higher satisfaction levels than modern marriages.
Gen Z will have the strongest marriages, though fewer of them.
Women just began to have a bit of choice in relationships in the 1960's.
Wanting physical attraction is still important. But, the willingness and desire to be authentic is even more important.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett -  Paul Brunson: "The 70/30 Body Shape Is Scientifically The Most Sexy", "The ONE Question That Predicts Divorce!" & "The Link Between Childhood Trauma & Sex"
